title: Phyteuma corniculatum
intro: Phyteuma corniculatum, also known as snake's head or cornflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Phyteuma corniculatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed in traditional medicine to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Phyteuma corniculatum is classified as a species within the plant family Campanulaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bellflowers. It is native to the mountainous regions of central and eastern Europe.
